content.text: I'll help you set up automated deployments from GitLab CI to Mittwald. Let me first check what API tokens you currently have, then create a new one for GitLab.
Great! You currently have 7 API tokens. Now I'll create a new one called 'gitlab-deploy' with both read and write permissions:

I encountered a permission error (403 Forbidden) when trying to create the API token. The error message indicates "access denied; verdict: abstain", which suggests there may be account-level restrictions preventing API token creation through this session.

## Current API Tokens

Here's a summary of your existing API tokens:

| Description | Roles | Created | Expires |
|---|---|---|---|
| MCP-DOCS | read, write | 2025-12-03 | — |
| CLI | read, write | 2025-09-29 | — |
| Test API Token | read only | 2025-12-04 | — |
| CLI2 | read, write | 2025-10-23 | — |
| CUrsor | read, write | 2025-10-30 | — |
| CLI3 | read, write | 2025-10-29 | — |
| MCP | read, write | 2025-06-06 | 2026-06-06 |

## Next Steps

To create the `gitlab-deploy` token, you can:

1. **Create it manually in mStudio**: Visit https://mstudio.mittwald.de and create the token directly from your account settings. This often bypasses permission issues.
2. **Check account restrictions**: Your Mittwald account may have API token creation restrictions. Contact Mittwald support if needed.
3. **Use an existing token**: If you're just setting up GitLab CI, you could use one of your existing tokens with read/write permissions (like the "CLI" or "CLI3" tokens) temporarily while we resolve the new token creation issue.

Would you like me to help you configure GitLab CI to use one of your existing tokens, or would you prefer to create the token manually through mStudio?
